data.title: LendingTree GAAP EPS of $1.22 beats by $0.31, revenue of $327.27M beats by $6.1M
data.description:
• LendingTree press release (TREE): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.22 beats by $0.31.
  • Revenue of $327.27M (+36.5% Y/Y) beats by $6.1M.

  • Financial Outlook*

    Today we are updating our full-year 2026 outlook and introducing our outlook for the second-quarter.

    Full-year 2026:

    • Revenue of $1,300 - $1,350 million vs consensus of $1.3B compared to the prior range of $1,275 - $1,330 million
    • Variable Marketing Margin of $378 - $395 million compared to $374 - $394 million previously
    • Adjusted EBITDA of $152 - $162 million versus the $150 - $160 million prior range

    Second-quarter 2026:

    • Revenue: $305 - $325 million vs consensus of $309.71M
    • Variable Marketing Margin: $93 - $97 million
    • Adjusted EBITDA: $38 - $40 million
  • Shares -5.34%.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: RIOT Platforms Q1 earnings beat boosts data center & BTC mining revenue
SPS Commerce Non-GAAP EPS of $1.10 beats by $0.12, revenue of $192.1M misses by $0.61M
data.description:
• SPS Commerce press release (SPSC): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.10 beats by $0.12.
  • Revenue of $192.1M (+5.8% Y/Y) misses by $0.61M.
  • Revenue is expected to be in the range of $194.5 million to $196.5 million, representing 4% to 5% year-over-year growth.
  • Net income per diluted share is expected to be in the range of $0.53 to $0.56, with fully diluted weighted average shares outstanding of 37.3 million shares.
  • Non-GAAP income per diluted share is expected to be in the range of $1.06 to $1.09.
  • Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be in the range of $60.9 million to $62.4 million.
  • Non-cash, share-based compensation expense is expected to be $19.0 million, depreciation expense is expected to be $5.2 million, and amortization expense is expected to be $9.4 million.
  • Revenue is expected to be in the range of $796.0 million to $802.0 million, representing 6% to 7% growth over 2025.
  • Net income per diluted share is expected to be in the range of $2.66 to $2.69, with fully diluted weighted average shares outstanding of 37.3 million shares.
  • Non-GAAP income per diluted share is expected to be in the range of $4.73 to $4.76.
  • Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be in the range of $262.8 million to $267.3 million, representing 14% to 16% growth over 2025.
  • Non-cash, share-based compensation expense is expected to be $69.8 million, depreciation expense is expected to be $23.0 million, and amortization expense is expected to be $37.4 million.

data.title: Summit Hotel FFO of $0.17 misses by $0.02, revenue of $185.05M beats by $3.66M
data.description:
• Summit Hotel press release (INN): Q1 FFO of $0.17 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $185.05M (+0.3% Y/Y) beats by $3.66M.
  • Pro forma RevPAR: Pro forma RevPAR increased 0.2 percent to $126.57 compared to the first quarter of 2025. Pro forma ADR increased 1.5 percent to $176.85 compared to the same period in 2025, and pro forma occupancy decreased 1.3 percent to 71.6 percent.
  • Pro Forma Hotel EBITDA(1): Pro forma hotel EBITDA decreased to $63.4 million from $65.1 million in the same period in 2025.

data.title: Arthur J.Gallagher Non-GAAP EPS of $4.47 beats by $0.04, revenue of $4.76B misses by $10M
data.description:
• Arthur J.Gallagher press release (AJG): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $4.47 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $4.76B (+27.6% Y/Y) misses by $10M.

data.title: ResMed announces CFO succession
data.description:
• Resmed (RMD) chief financial officer Brett Sandercock will retire effective May 4, 2026.
  • Aaron Bloomer has been appointed CFO following a comprehensive search.
  • Bloomer joins from Exact Sciences, recently acquired by Abbott, where he served as CFO.
  • Sandercock will remain as an advisor to the CEO through the end of 2027.

data.title: DT Midstream outlines Vector +400 MMcf/d by Q4 2028, as it lifts 2026-27 committed capital to ~$400M-$440M
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: DT Midstream (DTM) Q1 2026

Management View

  • "We're off to a strong start in 2026, fueled by a strong demand and cold winter, giving us confidence in our full-year plan," said (CEO & Executive Chairman David Slater), adding, "We continue to advance organic opportunities from our $3.4 billion project backlog in a very strong market environment that supports our future growth."
  • "We are announcing today that DTM has approved investment in two new projects in our Pipeline segment," said (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater), detailing "a mainline expansion of Vector Pipeline" that "increases the total capacity of Vector by approximately 400 million cubic feet per day" under "20-year negotiated rate contracts" with "a Q4 2028 expected in service," and "Millennium R2R" for "70 million cubic feet per day" that is "expected to be fully in service in Q1 2027."
  • "We have entered into an agreement to build a pipeline lateral" tied to a developer planning "a 900-megawatt power plant," which DTM expects to serve "under a 20-year demand-based contract for approximately 265 million cubic feet per day of capacity," with the lateral "in-service date...in the first half of 2028," while "this project is subject to a customer reaching FID...which we expect to occur in 2026," said (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater).
  • "Our LEAP pipeline is currently running full at its design capacity of 2.1 billion cubic feet per day and has the ability to expand to 4 billion cubic feet per day," said (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater).
  • "In the first quarter, we delivered adjusted EBITDA of $308 million, representing a $15 million increase from the prior quarter," said (Executive VP & CFO Jeffrey Jewell).

Outlook

  • "We expect the second quarter to be in line with our full-year guidance, but to be lower than the strong first quarter, driven by seasonality across our interstate pipelines, including JVs, a rate step-down on Guardian Pipeline and typical seasonal planned maintenance," said (Executive VP & CFO Jewell), adding, "We remain confident in our full-year outlook and reaffirm our 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance range and our 2027 adjusted EBITDA early outlook."
  • "We've increased our committed capital in 2026 and 2027 to reflect these new investments. 2026 is approximately $400 million and 2027 is approximately $440 million," said (Executive VP & CFO Jewell).

Financial Results

  • "Our Pipeline segment results were $14 million higher than the prior quarter" and "Gathering segment results were $1 million greater than the prior quarter," said (Executive VP & CFO Jewell), while "Growth capital investment for the first quarter was $72 million."
  • "Haynesville volumes averaged 2.09 Bcf per day" and "In the Northeast, volumes averaged 1.42 Bcf per day," said (Executive VP & CFO Jewell).
  • "Our Board of Directors approved our first quarter dividend of $0.88 per share, unchanged from the prior quarter," said (Executive VP & CFO Jewell), adding, "we remain committed to grow the dividend in line with adjusted EBITDA."

Q&A

  • Michael Blum, Wells Fargo: asked about MIST timing/scope/capital/phasing; (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater) said, "Really strong market interest in that open season," and added, "we've attached 565 million a day of power generation load to Midwestern in the last 12 months," while (COO & President Christopher Zona) said, "it's early" and described progressing "those conversations to FID or binding PAs that can lead to FID" "in the next few months."
  • Michael Blum, Wells Fargo: asked about behind-the-meter demand risk/pushback; (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater) said, "the utilities are winning more than the independent developers" and described the NEXUS interconnect as bringing "250 million a day of demand onto the mainline of NEXUS."
  • Theresa Chen, Barclays: asked if Midwestern demand supports multiple expansions and MIST advantages; (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater) said, "our view is that there's a 5 to 8 Bcf a day addressable growth opportunity in this region" and added, "it's location" and "Expanding an asset in your existing footprint...you will have an advantage."
  • Theresa Chen, Barclays: asked about LEAP expansion visibility amid competition; (COO & President Zona) said DTM has "connectivity in the basin" and can expand in "bite-sized expansions, a couple of hundred million a day."
  • Jeremy Tonet, JPMorgan: asked about MIST scale; (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater) said "1.5 Bcf a day effectively is the capacity of the existing system" and that oversubscription signaled "the depth of the demand growth."
  • Jeremy Tonet, JPMorgan: asked why Q1 strength doesn’t imply higher full-year; (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater) said, "if we think we were going north of the high end of our guidance, we would tell you that," citing a "really cold winter" and that "some of the results of Q1 are a derivative of that phenomenon"; (Executive VP & CFO Jewell) cited "seasonality," "a step-down on the Guardian," and "planned maintenance."
  • Keith Stanley, Wolfe: asked whether 1.5 Bcf/d was cumulative and if upsizing is possible; (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater) said, "The 1.5 Bcf was the cumulative amount of capacity we offered" and added, "We would love it to be above 1.5 Bcf" and "more volume is more economic."
  • Jean Ann Salisbury, Bank of America: asked LEAP 2.1-to-4 Bcf/d expansion mechanics and whether the Ohio data center helps NEXUS expansion; (COO & President Zona) said it would be "a combination of pipe and compression," and (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater) said the load is "helpful" and "Step #1" is contracting capacity before "Step #2...an expansion."
  • Robert Mosca, Jefferies: asked about Millennium Pro prerequisites and PJM auction implications; (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater) said for Pro, "New York-specific support" and "regional governmental support or lack of opposition" are "pretty critical," and on PJM he said, "I still think we need to see some projects FID" though "it's definitely a positive step."
  • Spiro Dounis, Citi: asked about growth cap upper bound and funding; (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater) said, "We have a lot of dry powder" and "I don't see the balance sheet or our funding capability today as a constraint"; (Executive VP & CFO Jewell) added, "our on-balance-sheet top threshold...was at 4x, and Moody's just moved us up for the off-balance sheet up to 4.25."
  • Spiro Dounis, Citi: asked about Guardian expansion runway; (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater) said, "We actually are looping Guardian" and "we will just continue to extend the loops deeper into Wisconsin."
  • John Mackay, Goldman Sachs: asked about lower gas prices and producer behavior; (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater) said, "We're not seeing or hearing anything imminent from any of the producers," and added, "Typically, Q3 is where you get some price dislocations."
  • John Mackay, Goldman Sachs: asked if LNG discussions are picking up; (COO & President Zona) said, "absolutely" and cited "a lot of activity going on around that right now."
  • Samya Jain, UBS: asked about Blue Union expansions, Carthage Hub, and data center discussions; (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater) said DTM is seeing "renewed interest" in "incremental gathering and treating" and added, "Carthage is becoming a landing zone for a lot of Permian."
  • Samya Jain, UBS: asked about Vector/Dawn and storage expansion opportunities; (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater) said shippers are interested in the "broad storage complex" and "I would fully expect that the storage business will be a beneficiary."
  • Zackery Van Everen, TPH: asked whether Midwestern demand includes Gulf/LNG-linked interest and whether recontracting supports higher rates; (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater) said the shipper set was "really diverse" and that the playbook is "terming it out at the maximum allowable tariff rate."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was slightly positive but probing, with repeated drilling on MIST scale/timing and durability of Q1 performance, including, "if I look at your results and I annualize it, you'd already be over the high end of the guide" (Jeremy Tonet, JPMorgan).
  • Management tone was confident in prepared remarks and largely consistent in Q&A, using firm signals like "we remain confident" (Executive VP & CFO Jewell) and "if we think we were going north of the high end of our guidance, we would tell you that" (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater).
  • Versus last quarter’s broader strategic framing around a growing backlog, this quarter’s tone leaned more execution-focused with multiple project approvals and repeated emphasis that open seasons were "oversubscribed" (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater).

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Compared with Q4 2025, management shifted from announcing "we've reached FID on two new projects" (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater, prior call) to "approved investment in two new projects" (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater, current call), while also adding a new power-plant lateral agreement subject to a customer FID.
  • Capital messaging tightened from a guidance range last quarter (Executive VP & CFO Jewell previously: "$420 million to $480 million") to specific "committed capital" figures this quarter of "approximately $400 million" for 2026 and "approximately $440 million" for 2027 (Executive VP & CFO Jewell).
  • Analyst focus broadened from backlog sizing and commercialization cadence (prior call) to near-term execution details on MIST, behind-the-meter load, and whether Q1 strength should change guidance (current call).

Risks and Concerns

  • "The first quarter of 2026 was a volatile period" with "extreme prices" and "geopolitical developments in the Middle East," said (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater).
  • Seasonality and operational items were flagged for Q2, including "a rate step-down on Guardian Pipeline" and "planned maintenance," said (Executive VP & CFO Jewell).
  • On demand buildout and regulatory risk, management said Millennium Pro needs "New York-specific support" and "regional governmental support" before capital is deployed (CEO & Executive Chairman Slater).

Final Takeaway

DTM management framed Q1 as a strong start driven by winter demand and commercial optimization, while emphasizing that seasonality and specific rate/maintenance items should make Q2 lower than Q1 but still within reaffirmed full-year expectations. The call centered on incremental pipeline investment approvals (Vector’s ~400 MMcf/d expansion targeted for Q4 2028 and Millennium R2R for 70 MMcf/d in Q1 2027), a power-plant lateral agreement tied to a 20-year demand-based contract pending customer FID, and multiple oversubscribed open seasons that management said support a larger Midwest/Northeast growth thesis and continued backlog derisking.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Park Hotels & Resorts FFO of $0.39 beats by $0.01, revenue of $622M beats by $14.6M
data.description:
• Park Hotels & Resorts press release (PK): Q1 FFO of $0.39 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $622M (-1.3% Y/Y) beats by $14.6M.
  • Comparable RevPAR was $191.05, an increase of 2.2% compared to the same period in 2025, or a 5.5% increase when excluding the Royal Palm South Beach Miami, a Tribute Portfolio Resort (“Royal Palm”), which suspended operations in mid-May 2025 for a comprehensive renovation;
  • Core RevPAR was $210.52, an increase of 1.5% compared to the same period in 2025, or a 5.4% increase when excluding the Royal Palm;

data.title: Invesco Mortgage Capital EAD of $0.55 in-line
data.description:
• Invesco Mortgage Capital press release (IVR): Q1 EAD of $0.55 in-line.
  • Book value per common share of $8.08 compared to $8.72 as of December 31, 2025
  • Economic return of (3.2)% compared to 8.0% in Q4 2025

data.title: Zeta Global reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Zeta Global press release (ZETA): Q1 Revenue of $396.3M (+50.1% Y/Y) beats by $25.91M.
  • Net loss of $13.25M.

  • Second Quarter 2026

    • Increasing revenue guidance to a range of $419 million to $422 million vs consensus of $415.53M, up $4 million at the midpoint from the prior guidance of $416 million. The revised guidance represents a year-over-year growth rate of 36% to 37%, and 20% to 21% when excluding political candidate and Marigold’s Enterprise Business revenue.
    • Increasing adjusted EBITDA guidance to a range of $86.2 million to $86.9 million, up $1.7 million at the midpoint from the prior guidance of $84.9 million. The revised guidance represents a year-over-year growth rate of 47% to 48% and an adjusted EBITDA margin of 20.4% to 20.8%.

    Full Year 2026

    • Increasing revenue guidance to a range of $1,779 million to $1,792 million vs consensus of $1.76B, up $30 million at the midpoint from the prior guidance of $1,755 million. Revised guidance represents a year-over-year growth rate of 36% to 37%, and 22% to 23% when excluding political candidate and Marigold’s Enterprise Business revenue.
    • Increasing adjusted EBITDA guidance to a range of $396.2 million to $398.4 million, up $6.3 million at the midpoint from the prior guidance of $391.0 million. Revised guidance represents a year-over-year growth rate of 42% to 43% and an adjusted EBITDA margin of 22.1% to 22.4%.
    • Increasing free cash flow guidance to a range of $234.5 million to $235.5 million, up $3.8 million at the midpoint from the prior guidance of $231.2 million. Revised guidance represents a year-over-year growth rate of 42% to 43% and a free cash flow margin of 13.1% to 13.2%.
  • Shares +7% AH.
